touches.ai_summary: Zara Singh from BoldSpark submitted an inbound demo request via website form. Interested in call recording and coaching capabilities for their sales team. Currently evaluating Chorus as well.
Follow-up email sent to Zara confirming receipt of demo request and proposing three time slots for discovery call. Zara responded confirming Thursday 2pm works for her schedule.

questions_asked.question: Will we be able to see the coaching workflows in the demo?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, we'll walk through the full coaching cycle including call review, scorecard creation, and rep feedback loops.

touches.ai_summary: Pre-call email sent with discovery agenda and questions about current coaching process, team size, and goals. Zara replied confirming agenda looks good and mentioned they have 6 reps who would use the platform.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for coaching and call reviews?
questions_asked.answer: Very manual right now - managers listen to calls when they have time, but there's no systematic approach. We're missing a lot of coaching opportunities.

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Zara covered current pain points with manual call review process, team structure (6 sales reps, 1 manager), and coaching goals. Zara is Head of RevOps and owns sales enablement initiatives. Currently reviewing Chorus as alternative. BoldSpark is a professional services firm with 82 employees looking to scale coaching without adding headcount. Timeline is flexible but would like to decide within 4-6 weeks.

questions_asked.question: How does your conversation intelligence compare to Chorus? We've been looking at them as well.
questions_asked.answer: Both platforms do call recording and transcription well. Our differentiation is in the coaching automation - we can auto-generate scorecards based on your methodology and route specific call moments to managers for review.

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in the evaluation and decision process?
questions_asked.answer: I'll be driving this, but I'll need to loop in our VP of Sales eventually for final approval. Right now it's just me doing the initial research.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are up and running within 2 weeks - we handle the technical setup and then do coaching methodology workshops with your team.

objections: Need to understand pricing before bringing in leadership

touches.ai_summary: Meeting to review proposal and platform capabilities. Zara walked through specific coaching use cases. Discussion focused on unlimited automation tier vs pay-per-seat model. Zara mentioned timing is getting more urgent as Q2 ramp is coming. Still haven't involved VP of Sales but planning to soon.

questions_asked.question: Can you break down what's included in the unlimited automation tier?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited automated scorecards, call highlights, coaching insights, and custom playbook tracking. No caps on recordings or AI processing.

questions_asked.question: When would you ideally want to have this implemented?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally before our Q2 kickoff in three weeks, but we need to finalize budget and get leadership aligned first.

next_steps: Send formal proposal with 6 seats at unlimited tier
Zara to schedule meeting with VP of Sales next week

touches.ai_summary: Executive alignment meeting - Zara provided overview of evaluation to date. Discussed implementation approach and timeline. Conversation shifted to contract terms and budget constraints. Zara mentioned they're still in final discussions with Chorus and need to make a decision by end of week.

questions_asked.question: What does your onboarding support look like? Our team has limited bandwidth right now.
questions_asked.answer: We provide a dedicated implementation manager who handles all technical setup, plus 4 coaching methodology workshops. Average time investment from your team is about 5 hours total.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything blocking you from moving forward this week?
questions_asked.answer: Just need to compare final pricing with Chorus and get comfortable with the contract terms. Budget is approved but we want to make sure we're making the right choice.

objections: Chorus pricing came in lower than expected, need to understand value gap
next_steps: Zara to provide decision by Friday

touches.ai_summary: Pricing and terms call with Zara to address competitive pricing pressure from Chorus. Discussed discount options and payment flexibility. Zara emphasized budget is tight and they're looking at $5,500 ACV as the ceiling. Explored annual vs monthly commitment trade-offs.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Chorus on pricing or offer any discount to close the gap?
questions_asked.answer: We can offer 10% discount for annual prepay, which brings the 6-seat unlimited tier to $5,500 ACV. That includes full onboarding and ongoing support.

questions_asked.question: If we can hit that number, is there anything else you need to move forward?
questions_asked.answer: That pricing works within our budget. I'll need to run the final numbers by our VP, but assuming he's comfortable with the contract terms, we should be able to finalize this week.

next_steps: Send updated proposal at $5,500 ACV with annual terms
Zara to review with VP by Wednesday
Target signature by end of week
